All right, now let's put this whole thing together. You've learned all the formulas for creating these incredible pieces of marketing collateral. Now you have to execute. It's not going to be as difficult as you think. All you want to do is you want to wire frame your website first. Most of you don't even have to create a new website. You just need to cut most of the text off of your website. You want to clarify your language. You want to make sure it passes the grunt test. You want to make sure your header is offering enough information, all that kind of stuff. Make sure your buy now buttons are obvious and in the right place. Wireframe your website. That's the first step in the roadmap. Once that's done, I want you to create a lead-generating PDF. Remember everything that we said about the lead-generating PDFs. The title should be clickbait. The information that you offer should be really valuable. I get people saying sometimes, Don, I tried this and it didn't work for me. I look at their website and there's no text on their website or their images are confusing. Then their lead-generating PDF is a white paper with a really complicated title that nobody would want to download or worse, a really cute title that nobody can really understand what it's actually for. These are very easy problems to fix. If it's not working for you, I want you to clarify your language and make it more enticing because you're not following one of the rules because this stuff works across the board. You wireframe your website, then you create your lead-generating PDF. You should start working on that automated email campaign. You can create five or six emails that go out automatically every two or three days after somebody gives you their email address. That is going to see your sales increase by itself. When you add a sales letter using the pasture framework to that automated email campaign to close those sales, that's where you see a good uptick in sales. That's what we're all looking for. Those are the things that you want to get done right away. You sprinkle customer testimonials into that. You make sure your brand script is clarifying that message and it works. Here's how we've used it as story brand. We've told tons of people about this and we recommend you do the same thing. When I started the company, I realized people really don't think they need to clarify their message. They don't understand why. What they really want is a website that works. I created a lead-generating PDF. First, I wireframe my website really well. Then I created a lead-generating PDF called Five Things Your Website Should Include. I think in a relatively short period of time, 40,000 people downloaded it. Now, I have a little bit of a platform as far as I was able to tweet about it and Instagram about it and email some people about it. But 40,000 people downloaded that. That's 40,000 people now into my email sequence. You guys, it wasn't a very good sequence. We take months to create our email sequences now and they are amazing and they worked. In fact, I recommend if you're in one of ours, print them out and create a book. Don't steal my copy. You can create your own copy, but you can see how it's done well. We created some emails that are embarrassing looking back, but they built a $1.3 million company in one year. We were at a quarter million. We went to 1.3. I had to pivot from this Christian memoirist career to this story brand marketing career. It was a difficult pivot, but we did it with a lead-generating PDF and 10, might have been six emails. It wasn't very many emails. We saw that work and we did what you're going to do. We started tweaking it and making it better. We came up with another lead-generating PDF. Then we came up with a lead-generating video series called the Five-Minute Marketing Makeover. Thousands of people went through the Five-Minute Marketing Makeover. I think we saw a 60% increase in sales that year. Then we created another lead-generating video series called the Marketing Money Pit that a lot of you guys went through. It might be the reason they even bought this course. It's a marketing money pit followed by a series of emails that closes the deal. Whether or not you have a grocery store, whether or not you make furniture in your woodworking shop, whether or not you have a pet store, it doesn't matter. A wireframe website that really uses the right words to convert browsers to buyers, a lead-generating PDF, and an email sequence that closes with the sales letter should absolutely work to grow your business. It takes a little time to get that stuff right, but it'll absolutely work to grow your business. Listen, if you need some help, we actually certify coaches to help you execute the story around marketing roadmap. You can find those coaches at clarifyyourmessage.com. They're going to be a lot cheaper than other consultants, and they know how to make stuff that actually works. You can search for them there. Or you can just do it yourself. By the way, we don't get a percentage of any of the sales, the money that you give to our coaches. We don't get a percentage of any of that. They just pay to be certified, and they're good for the year. I have no vested interest in you getting this coach, but I do have a vested interest in this working. I have a vested interest in your company going from X amount of dollars to XXX amount of dollars. I want to see your company grow. If you don't execute the strategy, the strategy can't work for you. Put together a timeline and a schedule, and do these things that will grow your company. That is the story of brand marketing roadmap. Next time you see me, will you pull me aside and say, Don, we did this, and it worked great. Just talked to a guy, spoke at a conference in San Diego, and a guy pulled me aside and said, Don, I had an $800,000 business for five years. He did concierge medical services. Not exactly sure what that is, but it sounds kind of cool. $800,000 for four or five years running. We did basically executed the story of brand marketing roadmap after we clarified our message. One year, he did $2.3 million. That is obviously over 100% increase in revenue. I love hearing those stories. I take those stories back to my staff, and I say, you guys, we're helping wonderful people do wonderful things, but you've got to execute. Watch each of the videos, create each of the pieces of marketing collateral that we're talking about. If they're not working, tweak them, figure out why they're not working. It's going to be a lot of work, but once you create that campaign, the campaign still works. Final question. A lot of people say, Don, when do you kill a campaign? When do you let it go? Well, with the five things your website needs to include, I could have never killed that campaign. That campaign never stopped working. The only reason I created the next campaign was because I wanted to. I was bored with this campaign, and I wanted to create a new campaign. We had more money. We spent more money on creating it, and it was great. There are millions and millions of people out there who need your products and service. You're probably only selling to thousands of them a year. Some campaign can work forever. When do you kill a campaign? You kill it when it stops working. Or if you're like me, you kill it when you get bored, and you're having so much fun creating this stuff, you want to create something new. I wish you the absolute best. We love handing the microphone to good people and making sure they get the attention and that their company grows. If you're in our ecosystem, chances are statistically you're a really great person doing really great things for the world. Traces on hopefully finishing the StoryBand marketing roadmap, and now it's time to execute all the best to you.
